kpis.kpi_name: Describe the nature of financial management
kpis.definition: Financial management involves planning, organizing, directing, and controlling financial activities such as procurement and utilization of funds of an enterprise.
kpis.real_world_context: A business manager decides how to allocate budget between marketing, operations, and R&D to maximize profitability.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Students often confuse financial management with accounting. Accounting records what happened; financial management decides what should happen.
kpis.easy_angle: Define what financial management is and its main goals
kpis.medium_angle: Explain how financial management decisions affect business operations
kpis.hard_angle: Analyze a scenario where poor financial management led to business failure and identify what should have been done

kpis.definition: Financial statements are formal records of the financial activities of a business. The three main statements are the income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow statement.
kpis.formula: Net Income = Revenue - Expenses | Assets = Liabilities + Equity
kpis.real_world_context: An investor reads Apple's annual report to decide whether to buy stock. They check the income statement for profitability and the balance sheet for debt levels.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Profit does not equal cash. A business can be profitable on paper but still run out of cash if customers haven't paid yet.
kpis.easy_angle: Identify which financial statement shows revenue and expenses
kpis.medium_angle: Calculate net income or identify a line item from a provided income statement
kpis.hard_angle: Compare two companies' financial statements and recommend which is the stronger investment

kpis.definition: A cash flow statement shows how changes in balance sheet accounts and income affect cash and cash equivalents. It breaks down cash activity into operating, investing, and financing activities.
kpis.formula: Free Cash Flow = Operating Cash Flow - Capital Expenditures
kpis.real_world_context: A startup has $200K in sales but is running out of cash because customers pay on 90-day terms. The cash flow statement reveals this problem immediately.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Positive net income does not guarantee positive cash flow. Depreciation and accounts receivable timing can create large gaps.
kpis.easy_angle: Name the three sections of a cash flow statement
kpis.medium_angle: Identify whether a transaction belongs in operating, investing, or financing activities
kpis.hard_angle: Given a scenario with net income and working capital changes, calculate operating cash flow

kpis.definition: Financial ratios are mathematical comparisons of financial statement accounts that help evaluate a company's performance, liquidity, profitability, and solvency.
kpis.formula: Current Ratio = Current Assets / Current Liabilities | Debt-to-Equity = Total Debt / Shareholders Equity | ROE = Net Income / Shareholders Equity | Profit Margin = Net Income / Revenue
kpis.real_world_context: A bank uses the current ratio to decide whether to approve a business loan. A ratio below 1.0 signals the business can't cover short-term debts.
kpis.common_misconceptions: A higher ratio is not always better. A very high current ratio may mean the company is sitting on too much idle cash instead of investing it.
kpis.easy_angle: State the formula for a given ratio like current ratio or profit margin
kpis.medium_angle: Calculate a ratio from provided financial data and interpret what it means
kpis.hard_angle: Compare two companies using multiple ratios and recommend which is healthier

kpis.definition: Marketing is the activity, set of institutions, and processes for creating, communicating, delivering, and exchanging offerings that have value for customers, clients, partners, and society.
kpis.real_world_context: Nike doesn't just sell shoes — they market a lifestyle and identity. Their 'Just Do It' campaign speaks to aspiration, not product specs.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Marketing is not just advertising. It includes product development, pricing, distribution, and customer research.
kpis.easy_angle: Define marketing and distinguish it from just advertising
kpis.medium_angle: Explain how the four Ps of marketing work together in a real business
kpis.hard_angle: Evaluate a company's marketing strategy and identify gaps or improvements

kpis.definition: The seven marketing functions are: Distribution, Financing, Market Research, Pricing, Product/Service Management, Promotion, and Selling.
kpis.real_world_context: Amazon uses all seven functions: they research what customers want, price competitively, manage millions of products, promote through ads, sell directly, finance purchases with credit, and distribute globally.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Students often only think of promotion when they think of marketing. The other six functions are equally important.
kpis.easy_angle: Name and define the seven marketing functions
kpis.medium_angle: Match a business scenario to the correct marketing function
kpis.hard_angle: Analyze how a company failure relates to neglecting one of the seven functions

kpis.kpi_name: Explain the role of promotion in the marketing mix
kpis.definition: Promotion includes all communication methods used to inform, persuade, and remind customers about products. It includes advertising, personal selling, sales promotion, public relations, and direct marketing.
kpis.real_world_context: Coca-Cola spends billions on promotion to maintain brand awareness even though almost everyone already knows the product exists.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Promotion is not just about selling. It also builds brand awareness, changes perceptions, and maintains customer loyalty.
kpis.easy_angle: List the five elements of the promotional mix
kpis.medium_angle: Choose the most appropriate promotional method for a given business situation
kpis.hard_angle: Design a promotional strategy for a new product launch with a limited budget

kpis.kpi_name: Explain the nature of business law
kpis.definition: Business law encompasses the rules, statutes, codes, and regulations that govern commercial interactions and transactions. It provides a framework for conducting business ethically and legally.
kpis.real_world_context: When two companies sign a contract, business law determines what happens if one party doesn't deliver. Without it, business agreements would be unenforceable.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Business law does not just apply to large corporations. Every small business transaction, employment decision, and customer interaction is covered by some form of business law.
kpis.easy_angle: Define business law and explain why it matters
kpis.medium_angle: Identify which area of business law applies to a given scenario
kpis.hard_angle: Analyze a business dispute and recommend a legal course of action

kpis.kpi_name: Describe the nature of legally binding contracts
kpis.definition: A legally binding contract requires six elements: offer, acceptance, consideration, capacity, legality, and mutual assent (meeting of the minds).
kpis.real_world_context: If you order something online and the company confirms your order, you've formed a contract. If they don't ship, you can seek damages.
kpis.common_misconceptions: Verbal contracts can be legally binding too. Writing is not always required, but it makes contracts much easier to enforce.
kpis.easy_angle: Name the six elements required for a valid contract
kpis.medium_angle: Identify which element is missing from a scenario that makes a contract unenforceable
kpis.hard_angle: Analyze a complex contract dispute and determine whether a binding contract was formed
